name: In-vitro to in-vivo potency translation (power law)
description: Translates an in-vitro potency metric (e.g. unbound IC50, ng/mL) into an in-vivo drug-potency parameter for a TGI model: potency = scale * IC50^power (power=1 is linear scaling). A deliberately simple, transparent bridge for the discovery-to-clinic step; the assumption that in-vitro potency predicts in-vivo activity is itself the thing to validate (Rocchetti 2007). Values illustrative and unverified.

extraction.tier_rationale: Scale folds in protein binding, distribution, and assay-to-animal differences.
power=1 (linear) is the default; departures need cross-compound data.
